params.name: customer
params.description: Displays charges associated with the provided `customer ID`.

params.name: ending_before
params.description: Cursor used in pagination. The ending_before object ID sets your place in the list. For example, if you receive 100 objects in a list starting with obj_bar, add ending_before=obj_bar in your subsequent request to retrieve the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Additional information provided as an expanded response, for example, a related object nested within the parent. See [Use expandable fields](https://docs.clover.com/docs/expanding-fields).

params.name: limit
params.description:
Number of objects returned by the request, ranging between 1 and 100.
 Default: 10

params.name: threeds_validation_result
params.description: EMV® 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication result. 3-D Secure is a protocol that provides an additional security layer for online credit and debit card-not-present (CNP) transactions.

params.name: is_threeds
params.description: Indicates whether the transaction is 3-D Secure authenticated.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: Cursor used in pagination. The starting_after object ID sets your place in the list. For example, if you receive 100 objects in a list starting with obj_foo, add starting_after=obj_foo in your subsequent request to retrieve the next page of the list.

items.docs: Displays a list of existing charges. First displays the newest charge in the list. See [Get charges](https://docs.clover.com/docs/get-charges) tutorials for more information.
Charges a credit card or other payment source using data in the `charge` object. See [Create a charge](https://docs.clover.com/docs/create-a-charge) tutorial for more information.

params.name: chargeId
params.description: Universal unique identifier (UUID) of the charge to capture.

items.docs: As step two of the two-part payment flow, captures the payment of an existing but uncaptured charge. The first step is to [create a charge](https://docs.clover.com/reference/createcharge) with the capture option set to `false`.

params.name: chargeId
params.description: Universally unique identifier (UUID) of the charge.

items.docs: Retrieves the details of an existing charge. This information is also returned when a charge is first created, see [create a charge](https://docs.clover.com/reference/createcharge) or if it is refunded, see [create a refund](https://docs.clover.com/reference/createrefund).

params.name: charge
params.description: Retrieve refunds applied to a specific charge.

params.name: created
params.description: List filter based on the object's `created` field. The value can be a string with a Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) or a dictionary of multiple options describing a time range.

params.name: ending_before
params.description: Cursor used in pagination. The ending_before object ID sets your place in the list. For example, if you receive 100 objects in a list starting with obj_bar, add ending_before=obj_bar in your subsequent request to retrieve the previous page of the list.

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of objects returned by the request, ranging between `1` and `100`. The default value is `10`.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: Cursor used in pagination. The starting_after object ID sets your place in the list. For example, if you receive 100 objects in a list starting with obj_foo, add starting_after=obj_foo in your subsequent request to retrieve the next page of the list.

items.docs: Returns a list of all existing refunds. The newest refund is first in the list. The ten most recent refunds appear by default on the `charge` object.
Creates a new refund that must be associated with an existing charge. Refunds are applied to the `source` used for the original payment. Partial refunds are allowed up to the original charge amount.

params.name: refundId
params.description: Universal unique identifier (UUID) of the refund to retrieve.

items.docs: Returns details of a specific refund.

Creates a single-use token, which is a unique code that indicates credit card details. Use this token with endpoints like [create a charge](https://docs.clover.com/reference/createcharge) or [pay for an order](https://docs.clover.com/reference/postordersidpay).

> **Note:** To use this endpoint, please select the **API Sandbox server** (`https://apisandbox.dev.clover.com`) from the Base URL dropdown in the right panel.

Creates a hosted checkout payment object to connect an application. Use this endpoint to request a new checkout session for a customer transaction. For more information, see [Create a Hosted Checkout session request](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/creating-a-hosted-checkout-session).
